options: The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans

questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 5 m carries a UDL of 12 kN/m. The fixed-end moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The recommended camber for earth roads in heavy rainfall areas is:
questions.solution: Earth road camber: 3-4% heavy rain, 2-3% light rain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In prestressed concrete, the cross-section is usually:
options: Smaller than equivalent RC section for same load
questions.solution: PSC uses higher strength materials → smaller sections for same load capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the flexure formula M/I = σ/y = E/R, the term R represents:
questions.solution: R = radius of curvature of the beam's neutral axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 12 kN. The fixed-end moment at each support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The length of a transition curve based on the allowable rate of change of centrifugal acceleration varies with the design speed v as:
questions.solution: Ls = v³/(C·R), so the comfort-based transition length grows with the cube of speed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shrinkage strain for pre-tensioned members as per IS 1343 is:
questions.solution: IS 1343: ε_sh = 0.0003 for pre-tensioning, 0.0002/(log10(t+2)) for post-tensioning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=60$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 8 m carries a UDL of 15 kN/m. The fixed-end moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The viscosity grade of bitumen VG-30 is suitable for:
questions.solution: VG-30 = most common paving grade for Indian conditions (equivalent to 60/70).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The grade of concrete for prestressed work should be at least:
options: M40 for post-tensioned, M30 for pre-tensioned (IRC/IS)
questions.solution: Min concrete grade: M30 for pre-tensioned, M40 for post-tensioned (IS 1343).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 150 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 500 mm². The direct stress is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 10 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 10 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 10 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 4 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 10 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The critical rate of superelevation for mixed traffic is:
options: Limited to avoid slow-moving vehicle sliding
questions.solution: With mixed traffic, e is limited by slow vehicles (max 7% plain, 10% hilly).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum compressive stress in concrete at transfer is limited to:
questions.solution: At transfer: max compression = 0.5 f_ci (IS 1343) where f_ci = cube strength at transfer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a point load of 30 kN at 1 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times8^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loss of prestress due to relaxation of steel depends on:
questions.solution: Relaxation loss = function of initial stress/UTS and time (IS 1343 gives values).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the stress-strain curve of mild steel, the yield plateau is followed by:
questions.solution: Mild steel: elastic → yield plateau → strain hardening → necking → fracture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Kani's method is also known as:
questions.solution: Kani's = rotation contribution method (iterative, simpler for multistory frames).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The California Bearing Ratio test measures:
options: Penetration resistance of soil under controlled conditions
questions.solution: CBR = load required for 2.5/5 mm penetration / standard load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relaxation loss in steel depends on:
options: Type of steel, initial stress level, and temperature
questions.solution: Relaxation = time-dependent stress reduction at constant strain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The polar moment of inertia of a solid shaft of diameter D is:
questions.solution: J = πD⁴/32 for solid circular shaft.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 8 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The length of a runway is increased by about 7% per 300 m elevation above MSL due to:
options: Reduced air density affects lift and engine performance
questions.solution: At higher elevation, air density decreases → longer takeoff run needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The creep coefficient for concrete as per IS 1343 at 7 days loading is:
questions.solution: Creep coefficient θ: 2.2 at 7 days, 1.6 at 28 days, 1.1 at 1 year.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 10 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 5 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.stem: The error in levelling due to curvature of earth is:
options: 0.0785 d² (d in km, correction in m)
questions.solution: Curvature correction C_c = d²/(2R) = 0.0785 d² m (d in km).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2 m long carries a 8 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 8 × 2 = 16 kNm.

questions.stem: A cantilever 3 m long carries a 8 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 8 × 3 = 24 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 5 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 35°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 35°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+80\times\tan 35^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=76.02$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum shear stress to average shear stress in a rectangular beam section is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For domestic sewage, the typical BOD₅:COD ratio is:
questions.solution: BOD₅/COD ~ 0.5-0.6 for untreated domestic sewage (indicates biodegradability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 60 km/h and radius 400 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fore bearing of a line AB is 120°. The back bearing is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 400 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 400 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times300\times400^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 198.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times8}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=60$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Chezy coefficient C is related to Darcy-Weisbach friction factor f by:
questions.solution: C = √(8g/f) from equating Darcy-Weisbach and Chezy equations.

options: The survey lines form a closed polygon
The area is bounded by a river
questions.solution: Closed traverse = returns to starting point (loop) or connects known points.

questions.solution: A contour line connects points of equal elevation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For laminar flow in a circular pipe, the ratio of maximum to mean velocity is:
questions.solution: The parabolic profile gives Vmax/Vmean = 2.0 for a circular pipe (the 1.33 value would apply to a wide flat channel).

questions.solution: DGPS = differential correction yielding accuracy of 0.1-1 m or better.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 2 m is founded at depth 1 m. Soil: c=10 kPa, γ=18 kN/m³, Nc=25.1, Nq=12.7, Nγ=9.7. The ultimate bearing capacity (Terzaghi) is:
questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 654.2$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m clay layer (Cc = 0.3, e₀ = 0.6) under an initial effective stress of 120 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.3, H = 4 m, e₀ = 0.6, σ₀ = 120 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.3\times4000}{1+0.6}\log_{10}\tfrac{120+60}{120}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 132.1$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plasticity index of a soil is:
questions.solution: Plasticity Index = Liquid Limit − Plastic Limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The momentum correction factor for laminar flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: β = 4/3 for laminar flow, ~1.02-1.05 for turbulent flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 60 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+60\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=54.64$ kPa.

questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 1820.0$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear stress at the neutral axis of a rectangular beam is:
questions.solution: Shear stress is max at NA for rectangular sections (τ = VQ/Ib).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3D truss has how many equilibrium equations per joint?
questions.solution: 3D: ΣF_x = 0, ΣF_y = 0, ΣF_z = 0 (3 eqns per joint).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 8 m carries a UDL of 30 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 30×8²/2 = 960 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 100 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 30 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 100 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 30 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=42.43$; $\sigma_1=70+42.43$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=112.43$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2.5 m long carries a 5 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 5 × 2.5 = 12.5 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam is said to be statically determinate if the number of unknown reactions is:
options: Equal to the number of equilibrium equations
questions.solution: Determinate = number of reactions = number of equilibrium equations (3 for 2D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 5 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{5^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 1263.3$ kN.

questions.stem: Which IS soil classification symbol represents well-graded SAND?
questions.solution: S = sand, W = well-graded. SP = poorly graded sand, SM = silty sand, SC = clayey sand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loss of prestress due to elastic shortening is most significant in:
questions.solution: Elastic shortening loss is significant in pre-tensioning and sequential post-tensioning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam under a central point load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The bending moment is maximum at midspan.
The deflection is maximum at midspan.
The maximum bending stress occurs at the neutral axis.
The shear force is zero at midspan.
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly with distance from the neutral axis — it is zero at the NA and maximum at the extreme fibre, so that statement is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a point load of 20 kN at 2 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:
questions.solution: Taking moments about right: Rₐ × 6 = 20 × 4 → Rₐ = 80/6 = 13.33 kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 15 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 15 kN, a = 1 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=15$ kN; $M_{max}=15\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=15$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the rise and fall method is:
options: More accurate than the height of instrument method
questions.solution: Rise & fall = more accurate (checks each BS/FS difference). HI = faster but less checking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456 limit-state design, the maximum compressive strain in concrete at the extreme fibre at the ultimate limit state is:
questions.solution: The limiting concrete strain in flexure is 0.0035; 0.002 is the strain at the onset of the constant-stress (rectangular) portion of the stress block.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Maxwell-Betti reciprocal theorem states:
options: δ_ij = δ_ji (deflection at i due to unit load at j equals deflection at j due to unit load at i)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing a three-hinged and a two-hinged arch, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: A three-hinged arch is statically indeterminate to the first degree.
Support settlement induces no stress in a three-hinged arch.
A three-hinged arch is free of temperature stresses.
A two-hinged arch is indeterminate to the first degree.
questions.solution: A three-hinged arch is statically determinate (the third hinge supplies the extra equation), so calling it indeterminate is false.
